Uses of Class
org.moeaframework.core.indicator.Indicators

Packages that use Indicators
Package
Description
org.moeaframework.analysis.io
File formats used by the MOEA Framework analysis codes.
org.moeaframework.core.indicator
Collection of unary quality indicators for comparing the quality of non-dominated approximation sets.

  • Uses of Indicators in org.moeaframework.analysis.io

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static MetricFileWriter
    MetricFileWriter.append(Indicators indicators, File file)
    Opens the metric file in append mode.
    static MetricFileWriter
    MetricFileWriter.overwrite(Indicators indicators, File file)
    Opens the metric file in overwrite mode.

    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    MetricFileWriter(Indicators indicators, File file, MetricFileWriter.MetricFileWriterSettings settings)
    Constructs an output writer for writing metric files to the specified file.

    Methods in org.moeaframework.core.indicator that return Indicators
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static Indicators
    Indicators.all(Problem problem, NondominatedPopulation referenceSet)
    Creates an instance of this class that evaluates all performance indicators.
    Indicators
    Indicators.include(EnumSet<StandardIndicator> indicators)
    Enables the evaluation of the selected indicators.
    Indicators
    Indicators.include(StandardIndicator indicator)
    Enables the evaluation of the given performance indicator.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeAdditiveEpsilonIndicator()
    Enables the evaluation of the additive ε-indicator metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeAllMetrics()
    Enables the evaluation of all metrics.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeContribution()
    Enables the evaluation of the contribution metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeGenerationalDistance()
    Enables the evaluation of the generational distance metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeGenerationalDistancePlus()
    Enables the evaluation of the generational distance plus metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeHypervolume()
    Enables the evaluation of the hypervolume metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeInvertedGenerationalDistance()
    Enables the evaluation of the inverted generational distance metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeInvertedGenerationalDistancePlus()
    Enables the evaluation of the inverted generational distance plus metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeMaximumParetoFrontError()
    Enables the evaluation of the maximum Pareto front error metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeR1()
    Enables the evaluation of the R1 indicator.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeR2()
    Enables the evaluation of the R2 indicator.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeR3()
    Enables the evaluation of the R3 indicator.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeSpacing()
    Enables the evaluation of the spacing metric.
    Indicators
    Indicators.includeStandardMetrics()
    Enables the evaluation of all standard metrics.
    static Indicators
    Indicators.of(Problem problem, NondominatedPopulation referenceSet)
    Creates a new instance of this class with no configured indicators.
    static Indicators
    Indicators.standard(Problem problem, NondominatedPopulation referenceSet)
    Creates an instance of this class that evaluates all standard performance indicators.
    Indicators
    Indicators.withEpsilons(Epsilons epsilons)
    Sets the ε values used by the indicators.
    Indicators
    Indicators.withHypervolume(double[] referencePoint)
    Configures the hypervolume metric using the given reference point.
    Indicators
    Indicators.withHypervolume(double[] minimum, double[] maximum)
    Configures the hypervolume metric using the given minimum and maximum bounds of the Pareto set.
    Indicators
    Indicators.withSubdivisions(int subdivisions)
    Sets the number of subdivisions used by the R-indicators.
